standard_variable.semantic_intent: source-supplied ICU first-day OASIS score summary

database_mapping.source_time_field: starttime + endtime or ICU intime-derived first-day window
database_mapping.source_grain: one official source-supplied OASIS row per stay_id
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained OASIS score summary row per stay_id

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patient identifier joined and checked against icu.icustays
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: hospital admission identifier joined and checked against icu.icustays
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: ICU stay identifier from source OASIS row and icu.icustays

score_phenotype_build_translation.time_basis_translation: retain source starttime/endtime when supplied, otherwise retain the first 24 hours after ICU intime as the OASIS score window
score_phenotype_build_translation.score_value_translation: map source oasis to std_oasis_total with canonical unit score_points and score-range validation
score_phenotype_build_translation.component_trace_translation: retain official hospital mortality probability and OASIS component subscores as same-row trace context
score_phenotype_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a row means no approved source OASIS row under this mapping, not low severity

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not universal proof of low severity and should not be converted to zero OASIS without a governed downstream rule
notes: This mapping preserves the source-supplied OASIS score rather than recalculating it from raw events.
Some component subscores can be null while the primary total and mortality probability remain required.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_oasis outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
